claim: Explorer evidence is valid: boundary snapshot audit implementation exists with git ls-files coverage, walkFiles fallback, artifact root exclusion, mutation detection, and test coverage.

evidence.summary: gitFiles implementation confirmed: runs 'git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z' for tracked/cached/other files
evidence.why: Primary evidence supports claim that git-backed boundary audit covers tracked and untracked working tree files

evidence.summary: walkFiles fallback confirmed: excludes .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache
evidence.why: Evidence supports claim that non-git projects have fallback file walk with reasonable defaults

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot confirmed: uses gitFiles with walkFiles fallback and excludes allowedRoots
evidence.why: Evidence supports claim that artifact roots are excluded from snapshot to prevent false violations

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots confirmed: compares before/after maps for added/deleted/modified files
evidence.why: Evidence supports claim that mutations are detected via snapshot comparison

evidence.summary: Runtime boundary snapshot capture confirmed: before/after agent dispatch with violation reporting
evidence.why: Evidence supports claim that boundary audit is enforced during agent execution

evidence.summary: Git-backed boundary test confirmed: validates untracked mutation detection via git ls-files -co
evidence.why: Test evidence confirms implementation behavior for tracked/untracked files

evidence.summary: Non-git fallback test confirmed: validates walkFiles detects modifications and ignores artifacts
evidence.why: Test evidence confirms fallback behavior and artifact exclusion work as claimed

risks: Git ls-files -co flag behavior not verified against git documentation for edge cases
SHA256 collision risk assumed negligible without cryptanalysis evidence
Excluded directory list may not cover all project-specific build/cache directories
Race condition risk from rapid file changes during snapshot not addressed

result.notes: All 7 evidence records have valid project scope, correct source types, exact paths, line ranges, and relevant summaries. Evidence supports the claim. Explorer correctly identified 4 assumptions and 4 risks rather than promoting them to facts. No evidence conflicts detected. PRD readiness criteria are met: project facts are verified from code and tests, assumptions are clearly separated, and acceptance criteria (boundary snapshots covering git/walk files with artifact exclusion) have evidence.
